notes.description: Golden expected output for the Space Colony Planning constraint solver scenario. Each step's expected values are derived from the hard constraints, objectives, and variable domains in problem.json. Only one objective is defined, so Step 3 (multi-objective) is skipped.

multi_objective.note: Skipped: only one objective defined (maximize_colonists). The Go example prints 'Only one objective defined; skipping multi-objective step.'

colonists.note: Binding constraint: solar_panels >= colonists * 0.3 with max solar_panels=200. 200 >= 666 * 0.3 = 199.8 (satisfied). 200 >= 667 * 0.3 = 200.1 (violated). So max colonists = 666.

has_landing_pad.note: Not constrained by objective or hard constraints

oxygen_ratio.note: oxygen_ratio * 666 >= 100, so oxygen_ratio >= 0.15; domain min 0.5 is binding

solar_panels.note: Maximized to domain upper bound to support max colonists

optimization.note: Single-objective: maximize colonists. The binding constraint is power_requirement: solar_panels >= colonists * 0.3. With max solar_panels=200, max colonists = floor(200/0.3) = 666.

satisfaction.note: Any feasible assignment satisfying all 5 hard constraints. Example: colonists=50, habitat_modules=5, solar_panels=20, water_recyclers=2, food_domes=1, oxygen_ratio=2.0, has_landing_pad=false. Exact values are solver-dependent.

soft_constraints.note: Soft constraint has_landing_pad==true (weight=3.0) is trivially satisfiable since the boolean variable is unconstrained by hard constraints. Single objective (maximize_colonists) persists from Step 2. Solver should satisfy the soft constraint without violation.

key_observations.colonists_note: solar_panels * 0.7 >= colonists * 0.3 tightens power constraint. With max solar_panels=200: 200*0.7=140 >= colonists*0.3, so colonists <= 140/0.3 = 466.67, max colonists = 466.
key_observations.colonists_reduction: From 666 to 466 (30% reduction, matching 30% panel efficiency loss)

Solar Storm Scenario.note: Solar storm reduces panel efficiency by 30%. The tighter power constraint (solar_panels*0.7 >= colonists*0.3) reduces max colonists from 666 to 466. This is pushed/popped so the base model is restored afterward.
